context: Tests whether the agent selects the 'perf' type for a performance improvement, writes a body that explains why the change was made (not what files were changed), wraps body lines at 72 characters, and separates the body from the header with a blank line.

checklist.name: Body present
checklist.description: The commit message includes a body section (not just a one-liner header)

checklist.name: Blank line separates header/body
checklist.description: There is exactly one blank line between the header and the body

checklist.name: Body explains why
checklist.description: The body explains the motivation (e.g. high latency, unbounded queries) rather than listing which files were changed

checklist.name: No file names in header
checklist.description: The header does NOT mention specific file names (e.g. 'search.ts', 'redis.ts')

checklist.name: Body lines ≤ 72 chars
checklist.description: Every line in the body section is 72 characters or fewer

checklist.name: Imperative header
checklist.description: The header description uses imperative mood (e.g. 'cache', 'add', 'reduce') not past tense

checklist.name: Header ≤ 72 chars
checklist.description: The header line is 72 characters or fewer

checklist.name: Lowercase description
checklist.description: The description portion of the header (after the colon and space) begins with a lowercase letter
